Yulang `v0.1.0-alpha.9` is an alpha release focused on typed error
aggregation and runtime oracle cleanup.

Contract v0 stable-core and the Contract v1 standard/resource slices remain
closed. This release promotes generated error helpers into the stable Standard
API contract: `error E:` declarations generate `fail`, `E::wrap`, explicit
`E::up`, `from`-registered casts, and `Display` output, and concrete
`from`-registered error effects can now satisfy annotated outer error effects
automatically at annotation boundaries.

## Highlights

- Added automatic annotation-boundary error upcasts. If a body produces a
  concrete `fs_err` effect and the annotated boundary requires a concrete
  `io_err` effect, `io_err: ... fs from fs_err ...` now lets the checker insert
  the generated cast without an explicit `io_err::up` call. Row variables and
  row tails pass through unchanged, and a missing `from` relationship remains a
  normal effect mismatch rather than a silent conversion.
- Registered generated `from` / `up` relationships in the same cast table used
  by hand-written `cast(x: T): U = ...` declarations, then added focused
  runtime and diagnostics cases for the automatic path, the no-`from` failure,
  and the row-variable non-conversion case.
- Promoted the result/error helper surface into the Standard API contract
  evidence: `result`, generated error helper signatures, `wrap`, explicit `up`,
  `from`, generated `Display`, and the new automatic annotation-boundary rule
  are now documented as executable contract behavior.
- Renamed the old `control-vm` crate to `control-ir` and removed its
  interpreter/runtime surface. The crate is now limited to IR, lowering, and
  evidence structures rather than serving as an executable backend.
- Removed the `run --control-vm`, `debug control-vm*`, `run-control`,
  `run-control-std`, and `--compare-control` CLI/debug surfaces. Runtime
  comparison tests now use a mono-runtime route instead.
- Added a minimal mono-runtime host-effect run API and a `--compare-mono` CLI
  comparison path so the existing evidence-vm comparison coverage can keep
  running without the removed control-vm interpreter.
- Removed the archived prior-generation source tree from the repository and
  dropped an unused wasm source dependency, reducing release source volume.
- Hardened evidence runtime execution with tail-call looping, known-state ref
  fast paths, MarkedForce tail reentry coverage, runtime gauges, and focused
  long-loop regressions.
- Memoized control-evidence walks and cached embedded standard-library lowering
  prefixes, reducing repeated work in evidence planning, source-unit cache
  routes, and playground startup paths.
- Added the unstable `std::text::config` preview slice with cookbook examples,
  wasm embedding, playground coverage, and Contract v1 config evidence for the
  current key/value plus section-header behavior.
- Fixed parser/string/playground regressions around no-cache parser rest
  execution, long parser patterns, small string scanner dispatch, continuation
  worker reuse, and full-only playground standard-library imports.
- Refreshed README and language documentation for current role syntax, generated
  error helpers, automatic error upcasts, contract evidence counts, and the
  current runtime surface.

## Install

Unix-like systems:

```sh
curl -fsSL https://yulang.momota.pw/install.sh | sh -s -- --version v0.1.0-alpha.9
```

Windows PowerShell:

```powershell
Invoke-WebRequest https://yulang.momota.pw/install.ps1 -OutFile install.ps1
powershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\install.ps1 -Version v0.1.0-alpha.9
```

## Known Limitations

- The language and standard library remain alpha-stage and may still change.
- Automatic error upcast is intentionally narrow: it only converts concrete
  effect atoms at declared or annotated boundaries when a generated `from` /
  cast relationship exists. It does not convert abstract row variables or row
  tails, and it is not a global effect-unification mechanism.
- The removed control-vm interpreter is no longer a fallback/debug runtime in
  release builds. The default execution path remains the evidence VM, with
  mono-runtime comparison available for focused oracle coverage.
- `std::text::config` is an unstable preview slice, not a finalized INI or
  general configuration-file dialect.
- Server resource behavior remains the alpha startup slice from the previous
  release; HTTP adapters, natural termination, timeouts, backpressure, and the
  connect side remain follow-up work.
